name: J.R.R. Tolkien
title: Author & Philologist
bio: English writer, philologist, and academic, best known for 'The Hobbit' and 'The Lord of the Rings.' Professor of Anglo-Saxon at Oxford University.
faith: A devout Roman Catholic whose faith deeply influenced his literary works. He was instrumental in C.S. Lewis's conversion to Christianity. He described 'The Lord of the Rings' as 'a fundamentally religious and Catholic work.'

sources.title: J. R. R. Tolkien — Wikipedia
sources.note: Biography including his Catholic faith and its influence on his works

sources.title: The Letters of J.R.R. Tolkien
sources.note: Edited by Humphrey Carpenter. Contains letters where Tolkien describes LOTR as 'a fundamentally religious and Catholic work'

sources.title: Tolkien's faith and Middle-earth
sources.note: Wikipedia article on Christian themes in Tolkien's works

tagline: Created Middle-earth as a fundamentally religious and Catholic work
